source_pages.markdown: [复杂表格 Markdown 已回退，请以 content.json / table metadata 为准]

rows: ExternalId of the order, needs unique
Account Id of the merchant where the order will be created at

1 REFUND
2 GOODS_SERVICES
3 COMMISSION
4 ROYALTY_DIVIDENDS
5 LOAN_REPAY
6 TREASURY
7 RENT
8 OTHERS 9REJECT_RETURN
Currency of the funding used for the payoutSupported: USDT,USDC The crypto-crypto payout scenario only supports trading pairs of the same currency
Intended receipt currency Currently Supported: USDT, USDC,USD The crypto-crypto payout scenario only supports trading pairs of the same currency

Amount the merchant tends to pay. Implied responsibility: Recipient bears network fee, paymode of order will be RECIPIENT_BEAR
Note: Mutually exclusive with receiveAmount. Crypto coin: 3dpWhen the purpose is REJECT_RETURN, the precision will be consistent with the orig payment

Amount the beneficiary is expected to receive. Implied responsibility: Payer bears network fee, paymode of order will be PAYER_BEAR Mutually exclusive with payAmount . Crypto coin: 3dpFiat coin: USD:2dp When the purpose is REJECT_RETURN, the precision will be consistent with the orig payment
time units，mandatory if payCurrency is different from settleCurrency (e.g.,M、S )

Represented in minutes/seconds. mandatory if payCurrency is different from settleCurrency If timeUnit=S, lockTime
limit 1~60 If timeUnit=M,lockTime
limit 1~120
Optional reference or description of the order， character limit: 512
The participant details arrays of the order, provide at least one originator AND one beneficiary. If it is not provided during payout creation, you need to provide it when payout is confirmed.

Mandatory if settleCurrency = Crypto This must be provided during payout confirm if it has
not been provided during payout creation
Mandatory if settleCurrency = Fiat This must be provided during payout confirm if it has not been provided during payout creation

tables.fallback_html:
FieldRequiredTypeDescription
requestIdYstringExternalId of the order, needs unique
accountIdYstringAccount Id of the merchant where the order will be created at
purposeYenum1 REFUND 2 GOODS_SERVICES 3 COMMISSION 4 ROYALTY_DIVIDENDS 5 LOAN_REPAY 6 TREASURY 7 RENT 8 OTHERS 9REJECT_RETURN
payCurrencyYenumCurrency of the funding used for the payoutSupported: USDT,USDC The crypto-crypto payout scenario only supports trading pairs of the same currency
settleCurrencyYenumIntended receipt currency CurrentlySupported: USDT, USDC,USD The crypto-crypto payout scenario only supports trading pairs of the same currency
payAmountCdecimal stringAmount the merchant tends to pay. Implied responsibility: Recipient bears network fee, paymode of order will be RECIPIENT_BEAR
Note: Mutually exclusive with receiveAmount. Crypto coin: 3dpWhen the purpose is REJECT_RETURN, the precision will be consistent with the orig payment
receiveAmountCdecimal stringAmount the beneficiary is expected to receive. Implied responsibility: Payer bears network fee, paymode of order will be PAYER_BEAR Mutually exclusive with payAmount . Crypto coin: 3dpFiat coin: USD:2dp When the purpose is REJECT_RETURN, the precision will be consistent with the orig payment
timeUnitCstringtime units，mandatory if payCurrency is different from settleCurrency (e.g.,M、S )
lockTimeCstringRepresented in minutes/seconds. mandatory if payCurrency is different from settleCurrency If timeUnit=S, lockTime
limit 1~60 If timeUnit=M,lockTime
limit 1~120
memoNstringOptional reference or description of the order， character limit: 512
partyDetailsCArray of objectsThe participant details arrays of the order, provide at least one originator AND one beneficiary. If it is not provided during payout creation, you need to provide it when payout is confirmed.
cryptoAddressInfoCArray of objectsMandatory if settleCurrency = Crypto This must be provided during payout confirm if it has not been provided during payout creation
fiatAccountInfoCobjectMandatory if settleCurrency = Fiat This must be provided during payout confirm if it has not been provided during payout creation
origPaymentIdCstringMandatory if return rejected payment

rows: Identifies whether the party detail information belongs to a BENEFICIARY or an ORIGINATOR. Possible values - BENEFICIARY, ORIGINATOR Must include at leastOne originator AND one beneficiary

Identifies whether the party detail information belongs to an INDIVIDUAL or a COMPANY
Possible values - INDIVIDUAL, COMPANY
The uuid tied to this beneficiary or originator.
First name of the individual (required for INDIVIDUAL entity type) Character limit: 255 Allowed: • Alphanumeric characters • Chinese characters
Last name of the individual (required for INDIVIDUAL entity type) Character limit: 255 Allowed: • Alphanumeric characters • Chinese characters
Date of birth of the individual yyyy-MM-dd
Type of the relationship between the BENEFICIARY/ORIGINATOR and the creator of the payment. Possible values - SELF_OWNED, THIRD_PARTY

The
ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 code of the country that the party resides in. Unsupported country code: BY,IR,MM,KP,UA,US,SY,RU,CN,SG

Address of the originator/beneficiary of this account. Please ensure the address is validAllowed: • 255 max characters • Alphanumeric characters • Chinese characters • Unicode letters • Digits (0-9) • Spaces • Basic punctuation: . , ' - / # ( ) （ ） [ ]

rows: ExternalId of the order, needs unique.
The unique id of the order.
Account Id of the merchant where the order will be created at
The id of channel used for the order
Type of the order CRYPTO_TO_CRYPTO_PAYOUT: when payCurrency&settleCurrency of the payout order both cryptoCRYPTO_TO_FIAT_PAYOUT: when payCurrency is crypto &settleCurrency is fiat of the payout order
Status of the payout order CREATED,CONFIRMED,REVIEW,REVIEWED,PROC ESSING,COMPLETED,FAILED,REVERSED,REJECT ED

1 REFUND
2 GOODS_SERVICES
3 COMMISSION
4 ROYALTY_DIVIDENDS
5 LOAN_REPAY
6 TREASURY
7 RENT
8 OTHERS 9REJECT_RETURN
Currency of the funding used for the payoutSupported: USDT, USDC
Intended receipt currency Currently Supported: USDT, USDC,USD

PAYER_BEAR,RECIPIENT_BEAR Description: PAYER_BEAR: Payer bear channel fee (payAmount is provided during order creation) RECIPIENT_BEAR: Recipient bear channel fee (settleAmount is provided during order creation) When orderType is CRYPTO_TO_FIAT_PAYOUT， payMode is RECIPIENT_BEAR
Total Amount debited from merchant. Include payAmount+real-time deduct or post paidplatformfee
Amount the merchant tends to pay. When payAmount is provided, payAmount is used for conversion calculation. When receiveAmount is provided, payAmount is the result of conversion calculation.

Amount credited to beneficiary, including channel feesWhen payAmount is provided, settleAmount is the result of conversion calculation. When receiveAmount is provided, settleAmount is used for conversion calculation.
Amount the beneficiary is expected to receive, excluding channel fees
The times when this locked quote expires. You must create the transfer before this time.
The time when the record was last updated.
The time when the order is complete
Conversion details of the payment, not provided if no conversion involved
Fee details of the order. If there is no charge, return an empty array
<s>Channel Fee details of the payment, depending on paymode, this fee is deducted either from payer or recipient</s>

rows: It is an enumeration: PLAT_COIN_PAYOUT,PLAT_FIAT_PAYOUT It means the handling fee charged by the platform during a payout CHANNEL_COIN_PAYOUT,CHANNEL_FIAT_PAYO UT It means the handling fee charged by the channel during a payout

true: it means the fee deducted by real time false: it means the fee deducted by month

rows: The block chain transaction hashID if it is a COIN_PAYOUT
The amount paid by the user. Note: The ERC20 protocol supports 6 decimal places for USDC/USDT.

The currency used by the platform for settling funds to the account (e.g., if the channel type is FIXED_NORMAL, the settlement currency is the same as the payment currency; for other channel types, it may be a different currency, such as USD)
Other blockchain-related information. It is required when the status is 'PROCESSING' or any subsequent statuses.
Number of block confirmations. For example: 4

The number of blocks we consider necessary for this transaction to be permanently confirmed. For
example: 32
